source: V25 working screen iteration 4, Marrick answer-seeking turn 9
candidate: Write: “The blank’s caster at the weir-forge has not yet been shown.” I open the leat-keeper’s book beside the coin, leaving the striker unproved. “I read in the record that The leat-keeper's book is exact. Since the forge was shut, one hand alone has drawn the weir crucible and signed for its charcoal: Edony, the founder's widow, who stayed on in the forge cottage when the fires went out.” Verrell’s mint-yard claim now falters; does this book place the blank in Edony’s hand?

due_evidence.surface: The leat-keeper's book is exact. Since the forge was shut, one hand alone has drawn the weir crucible and signed for its charcoal: Edony, the founder's widow, who stayed on in the forge cottage when the fires went out.

world.title: The Light Shillings
world.setting: The Marrick trial-book, leat-keeper's charcoal book, false shilling, touchstone, and weir crucible lie before the assay.
world.question: Whose hand struck the false shillings passed at the Marrick fair?

public_turn.learner_move: What should I write next about whose hand cast the blanks at the weir-forge?
public_turn.pressure_target: The town has its founder ready: Verrell alone draws the mint-yard crucible, licensed to no one else since the old assay-master's day. Whatever metal is cast in Marrick, the town says, is cast by Verrell's hand.
public_turn.contrary_evidence: The leat-keeper's book is exact. Since the forge was shut, one hand alone has drawn the weir crucible and signed for its charcoal: Edony, the founder's widow, who stayed on in the forge cottage when the fires went out.
